# Stale leader falls behind, loses election, gets its log truncated when the
# new leader replicates to it.
#
# Four voters, no witness. Quorum = 3.

add-voters
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
v3: [a1]
v4: [a1]
----
v1: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v2: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v3: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

# v1 wins election.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2: yes
v3: yes
v4: yes
outcome: won

# v1 proposes two entries but does NOT replicate them.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) leader
v2: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v3: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) leader
v2: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v3: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

# v2 campaigns. v1 is dropped (partitioned). v2 wins with v3+v4.
# v1 is not contacted so it stays at term b, still believing it's leader.
campaign v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 campaigns at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
v3: yes
v4: yes
outcome: won

# v2 proposes an entry.
propose v2
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) leader
v3: [a1] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

# v2 appends to v3 only. Matches: [0, 3, 3, 0]. Quorum match = 0, not committed.
append v2 drop=(v1, v4)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: (dropped)
v3: ok
v4: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

# v2 appends to v3 and v4. Matches: [0, 3, 3, 3]. Quorum match = 3, committed.
append v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: (dropped)
v3: ok
v4: ok
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) committed=c3 leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1 c2 c3] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)

# v1 tries to campaign. It's at term b, so it campaigns at term c.
# Everyone already voted for v2 in term c, so v1 is rejected.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2: no, already voted for v2
v3: no, already voted for v2
v4: no, already voted for v2
outcome: lost

# v1 tries again at term d. Now the log-up-to-date check kicks in: v2/v3/v4's
# last entry c3 has a higher term than v1's b4, so they reject.
# Side effect: everyone bumps to term d, v2 loses leader status.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term d
v1: yes (self)
v2: no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
v3: no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
v4: no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
outcome: lost

# v2 must re-campaign to resume leadership after the term bump.
# v1's log ends at b4 which is not ahead of v2's c3 (term c > b), so v1
# grants the vote despite having more entries.
campaign v2
----
v2 campaigns at term e
v2: yes (self)
v1: yes
v3: yes
v4: yes
outcome: won

# v2 appends to everyone including v1. v1's divergent log [b2 b3 b4] gets
# truncated and replaced with v2's log [c2 c3 e4].
append v2
----
v2 appends through e4
v1: ok, replaced 3 entries
v3: ok
v4: ok
v1: [a1 c2 c3 e4] term=e cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v2: [a1 c2 c3 e4] term=e cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4) committed=e4 leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3 e4] term=e cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
v4: [a1 c2 c3 e4] term=e cfg=(v1 v2 v3 v4)
